bullets.text: Q3 CR 100.4 (4 pts above 96.0 target); YTD CR 95.6, still under the 96 goal; Personal Lines Q3 CR 100.2 (+14 pts YOY) as loss frequency normalized and costs accelerated; catastrophe losses added 6.4 CR points including Hurricane Ida.
Companywide YTD NPW up 15%; Q3 NPW up 13%; Personal Lines Q3 NPW up 7%; Commercial Lines Q3 NPW up 47% driven by freight market strength, TNC recovery, and trucking segment growth.

Companywide PIF growth 9% YOY; personal auto crossed 17.5 million PIFs milestone, adding ~1.3 million in the prior 12 months; Commercial Lines PIFs up 19% YOY; Robinson segment (auto+home) PIFs growing in low double digits.

Personal auto frequency sustained at pre-pandemic levels; accelerating average loss cost trends; Hurricane Ida costliest storm in Progressive history — property estimated ultimate losses $450M (38 pts property Q3 CR); retained losses after reinsurance above $200M per-event threshold.

YTD personal auto rate increases ~5%; slowing advertising to reduce expenses while raising rates; personal auto model 8.7 in 17 states (~40% of premium) showing improved conversion of multi-car and prior-insurance segments.

Q3 investment return 0.2%; portfolio duration reduced to 3.0 years; share repurchases executed as price dipped; flexible capital allocation maintained; strong capital position supports both growth and shareholder returns.

Commercial auto model 8.0 with new policy admin system in 19 states (~50% of CL premium); BOP expanded to 29 states (~55% of CL multiperil market) adding 7 states in Q3; BQX consumer platform expanded carrier/product/state combinations.

Industry facing regulatory scrutiny of rate increases after 2020 credits; supply chain constraints limiting vehicle replacement for customers; freight market conditions driving exceptional commercial auto growth across trucking and intermodal segments.

Mike Sieger (Claims President) and Jeff Charney (CMO) announced retirements for early 2022; Remi Kent hired as new CMO (20+ years experience from 3M); John Murphy promoted to Claims President; Lori Niederst moved to CRM President.

Property YTD CR 125.1; three major cat events in 2021 (Texas winter storms, TX/OK hail, Hurricane Ida) contributed $364M net losses (24 pts YTD property CR); non-weather losses and expenses performing better than forecast; non-renewal actions initiated in high-volatility states.
